concerns.text: The Independent Test for User Story 3 states: 'modifying the VAF threshold parameter (e.g., from [deferred] to [deferred])'. This is a logical contradiction; a test cannot modify a parameter from a placeholder to the same placeholder. The test must specify concrete values (e.g., 0.5% to 2.0%) to be verifiable, or the text must be removed as it describes an impossible test case.

Success Criterion SC-003 states: 'variation in correlation coefficients across the {[deferred], [deferred], [deferred]} sweep'. This contradicts the requirement in FR-005 and US-3 which explicitly define the sweep thresholds as {0.5%, 1.0%, 2.0%}. SC-003 fails to reference the defined values, rendering the success criterion untestable and inconsistent with the functional requirements.

The Assumption about power states: 'sample size available... (N [deferred])'. While [deferred] is allowed for empirical values, the phrasing 'N [deferred]' is syntactically awkward and inconsistent with the rest of the document which uses '[deferred]' as a standalone marker for the value itself (e.g., 'r ≈ [deferred]'). It should be 'N = [deferred]' or 'sample size (N) is [deferred]'.
